reviews.address: 7 Boundary St, London E2 7JE, United Kingdom
reviews.review_text: Very good food and service. Portions were good in size for the price.

reviews.address: 12 Hanbury St, London E1 6QR, United Kingdom
reviews.review_text: We had the red curry and of the best pad Thais we have ever had! Service was quick, and the size of the portions was considerable.

reviews.place_name: BKC - Biryani Kebab Chai - Marble Arch
reviews.address: 7 Edgware Rd, Tyburnia, London W2 2ER, United Kingdom
reviews.review_text: We came to this restaurant due to the good reviews but quality overall is not on par with the prices here. Lamb roganjosh had several pieces of meat that were difficult to eat. The grilled chicken only had two small thighs that were completely dry. Both dishes were 17 pounds, with very small portions, plus adding the service tax turned out to be expensive for the quality and quantity. Will not come back unfortunately.
reviews.owner_response:
Hi Javier Molina,

Thank you for your honest and thoughtful review — we’re really pleased to hear you enjoyed the food and service.

We understand your point regarding pricing and portion size. As a central London restaurant specialising in authentic Awadhi cuisine, our dishes involve skilled labour, long cooking processes, and carefully sourced ingredients, all of which shape the experience we offer.

We appreciate you dining with us and hope to welcome you back again soon. 

Warm regards, 
Team BKC

reviews.place_name: La Casa Agency | Comillas Real Estate, S.L.
reviews.address: Calle de Antonio López, 39, Carabanchel, 28019 Madrid
reviews.review_text: We sold a property through LA CASA and the service was excellent. They are serious and friendly people.
reviews.owner_response: Thank you, Javier! We're proud to have facilitated the sale of your premises with professionalism and kindness. We greatly appreciate your trust. It was a pleasure working with you!

reviews.address: 205/2 RDT Centre Road, Pilimathalawa 20450, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: As other guests comment in the reviews, the place initially looks nice but could be much cleaner and is overpriced. There were stains everywhere in the bathroom and when it rained there was water everywhere in the bathroom as there were probably some holes in the ceiling. In terms of pricing, the place is slightly overpriced, and is far away from Kandy itself (around 35-40 min in tuk tuk). We had several problems during our stay related to the overprice of goods and services that we did not find in other places we stayed in Sri Lanka. They clearly see that this place is for tourists and overprice everything. On our arrival, we asked for a tuk tuk trip that was around 1h and back, total around 2h30 and the price they gave us was 15000 LKR (absolute madness). As well, pricing on dinner and drinks is around twice of what it should be on a regular restaurant, and they even charge 10% service charge on top plus 3% credit card fee on everything. Though we claimed these practices were abusive, we only found that the manager threatened to report us on booking.

reviews.address: WFRR+VP, Maafushi, Maldives
reviews.review_text: Went here a couple of times and this is a good local place! Food is very nice, however the service is slightly poor and the toilets are dirty. Would recommend if you want to dine in Maldives for an acceptable price!

reviews.address: W09, Rahvashaa Hingun, Maafushi 08090, Maldives
reviews.review_text: We did the half day trip and loved it. It was a beautiful experience, we saw 8 turtles, 2 sharks and a coral reef. The team is very helpful and guide you through the full activity. Would recommend 100%!
reviews.owner_response:
Hello Javier,
We’re so happy to hear you loved the half-day trip! It’s fantastic that you saw so many turtles, sharks, and the coral reef. Thanks for your kind words about our team—they’ll be thrilled to hear your feedback.
We appreciate the recommendation and hope to welcome you back for more adventures soon!

reviews.address: Ziyaaraiy Magu Road, Maafushi 08090, Maldives
reviews.review_text: Bought a bottle of water at the store and it was 20 MVR.. what a scam

reviews.address: Burevi Magu, Gulhi 08070, Maldives
reviews.review_text: We stayed on the island for a day and tried the tuna and the curries here. It was delicious. Honestly slightly overpriced due to the mid high prices in the restaurant plus the 26% tax+service charge, but you cannot expect anything else from Maldives. Would recommend to anyone who seeks quality food.

reviews.address: Lot No 10488, Nirolhu Magu, Hulhumale 23000, Maldives
reviews.review_text: Fantastic dinner and service. We had one biryani plain rice and a chicken tikka masala (slightly spicy) and two type of naan and it was delicious. One of the best tikka masala’s I have ever had, normally I do not order it in Europe as it is normally with a lot of cream, and this one surprised me. The dishes are quite big so it can be fine for two people with one curry and one rice and two naan/other sides if you are not very hungry.

reviews.address: 22 Colombo St, Kandy 20000, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: The best coffee we tried in Sri Lanka, also fairly priced. Very good coffee setup, and vibes just like in any other European Specialty Coffee in Europe. The staff is very nice and knows well about the coffee they have and how/where it was taken from. We bought a bag of coffee to take and they gave us a free coffee!

reviews.address: Inamaluwa - Sigiriya Rd, Sigiriya, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text:
Fun restaurant with good food (we had a chicken wrap which was quite nice) and good environment to have a cocktail or a beer.

The only downside is that the service completely abandoned us at the end of the service and had to ask for the check 4 or 5 times.

reviews.address: Sigiriya, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: One of the best chicken curries we had on the trip. Only downside is that it took around 1h to prepare, but completely delicious and fairly priced.

reviews.address: 2Q4F+722, Unnamed Road, Tangalle, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: Cozy place inside the beach to order some juice or food. We had a pineapple juice and a coconut for a not so expensive price. The location is amazing.

reviews.address: Yaddehimulla Rd, Unawatuna 80000, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text:
Went here on a taco Tuesday and honestly there’s a mixed bag of opinions, but would recommend this place if you want something fancy and not very high priced, this is a very good place. To start with, we ordered 4 margaritas, which arrived 15 minutes late. The waiters didn’t seem to care to take us order at the start but then were really close and came by a couple of times to ask if everything was fine.

We ordered around 10 tacos, the nacho bowl plate and 6 margaritas on a taco Tuesday and spent around 24.000 LKR, therefore being slightly expensive, but the tacos were good. Overall I would certainly recommend the tacos we ordered (shrimp, birria and cochinita) but not the nacho bowl. Margaritas were nice, but as in all places in Sri Lanka, cocktails are overpriced vs what food costs.

reviews.address: 120 Yaddehimulla Rd, Unawatuna, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: Local kitchen in Unawatuna, a place where there are mostly touristy places. The chicken curry and the dhal coming with it are probably the best we tried in 10 days in Sri Lanka, so very happy we came! We ordered cheese rotti and chicken curry for 2 and it was more than enough, spending 3200 LKR plus a beer. If you are in Unawatuna and you want to run away from touristy front of beach lunches, this is your place!

reviews.address: 159 Devala Rd, Unawatuna, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: Great coffee and service. Definitely specialty coffee and not those coffees that claim to be and serve you Lavazza.

reviews.address: 125 DS Senanayake Veediya, Kandy 20000, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: Place is fantastic and the owners are extremely kind. We ordered three coffees and chicken sandwich and the food and coffees were delicious.

reviews.address: No"01 Ella - Passara Rd, Ella 90090, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: Good option for a quick and cheap curry at Ella!

reviews.address: 77Q7+QR, Tissamaharama, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: This place is a guesthouse from a local family that makes you feel welcomed since second 1. It is highly recommended to book the safari with them, as the owner of the house also has a safari tour company, with great cars and guides. We managed to book the safari at a price which was way below the average on other places, and spent 12h in the park with excellent curry lunch included. The safari was incredible, we saw 4 leopards (4!), one 25min from the entry. I suppose it’s a bit of luck, but the guide knew all of the places and possible tactics to try during those 12h. They offered to wash our clothes for a very reduced price as well and if you choose dinner included, they will prepare an excellent and abundant dinner for you.

reviews.address: Hiriketiya Beach Rd, Dikwella 81200, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: Completely recommended. Rooms are very clean, and well decorated. It has a rooftop pool with sunbeds to share between 6 rooms, so everytime we went we were the only guests there. Fair price, with delicious breakfast (slightly less food than in other places we have gone too but delicious).
reviews.owner_response:
Hi Javier,  
We are delighted to hear that you had an enjoyable stay, and thank you very much for taking the time to leave a review. We hope to see you again soon!

reviews.address: 2, 182 Thambiligahawatta, Dikwella 81200, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: Delicious katsu poke! After eating local food for 10 days, we were craving for something different and we got it here! So glad we had dinner on this place.
reviews.owner_response: Thank you so much for eating with us.

reviews.address: Rukaththana Estate, Naulla, Demodara, Ella 90090, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: The perfect place to relax in Ella. The rooms are very clean, breakfast is fantastic and of course, the view and the private pool in each cabin which is incredible. Really the most special place we have been to in our stay. Slightly long way from the Ella center, with a shortcut to a 30 min walk to the nine arch bridge (crossing the train tracks and going through a train tunnel). Only downside is that it needs a tuktuk to the main road which the place provides for free.

reviews.address: XQF8+RMH, Old Uda Pussellawa Rd, Nuwara Eliya 22200, Sri Lanka
Bandaranaike Mawatha, Nuwara Eliya 22200, Sri Lanka
8CXX+H3C, No 76, kurundankulama, 4th Lane, Kandy Road, Kurundankulama 50000, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: Spent one night in this place with my partner and the place is great for the price. The owner organized our pickup to the airport and delivery to the next city, and a tour to the sacred temples. The place is incredible, with a very nice pool and rooms that are clean and with a nice shower. The only downside is that the place is far away from every tourist attraction, so you will have to take a Tuk tuk.

reviews.address: wela Mada Road Kayanwala, Sigiriya, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text: Best thing on this place is the location and views, though if you are going out to have dinner, the road back home will be completely dark (on our way back some locals alerted of a wild elephant so beware!). The hotel is fine, but it could be slightly cleaner, especially the pool. Staff are welcoming. If you are going to stay in sigiriya for one or two days, this place is completely fine!

reviews.address: Block 27,Misthill, Badulla Rd, Nuwara Eliya 22200, Sri Lanka
reviews.review_text:
The staff here is super welcoming (even with a cup of tea and some cookies!) and the place is clean and cozy. You feel like at home. Breakfast is one of the best we have had on our trip (quantity of the food is amazing) and even you have options to choose from. For the price, you can’t ask for more!

The only downside of this place is that it’s certainly far away from both the city center and from the nanuoya station, so you will have to get a ride to see everything.

reviews.address: C. de Belén, 5, Centro, 28004 Madrid
reviews.review_text: Excellent service from our waiter Marco Antonio

reviews.address: C. Calatrava, 44, Casco Antiguo, 41002 Sevilla
reviews.review_text: Excellent value for money, and the portions are delicious. The bull is highly recommended.

reviews.address: Alameda de Hércules, 57, Casco Antiguo, 41002 Sevilla
reviews.review_text: Very good breakfast and at a good price.

reviews.address: Alameda de Hércules, 54, Casco Antiguo, 41002 Sevilla
reviews.review_text: €3.50 for a small beer. €3.20 for a glass of wine, only a third full. There are better places.

reviews.address: 179 Rue Sainte-Catherine, 33000 Bordeaux, France
C/Monte Paraíso 2, Urb Sitio de Calahonda, Mijas-Costa, Málaga, España, 29649 Mijas, Málaga
reviews.owner_response: Hi Javier, thank you so much for your rating! 🙏 …

reviews.address: C. Isla de Mouro, 1, 39140 Somo, Cantabria
reviews.review_text:
RECTIFYING IS WISE

EDITED REVIEW AFTER THIS YEAR'S CHANGES AT THE BAR

Bar Pepe has undergone a radical change in both service and prices, and will be revamped in the summer of 2023, becoming a similar but somewhat more modern version of its former self, with an exquisite tortilla pincho and delicious and enormous sandwiches. Prices have been reduced on some items, with the gin and tonic dropping from 9-10 euros to 6-7.

PREVIOUS REVIEW

I was a regular at the previous bar, and when I returned after the reopening, the prices have risen, also worsening the service. For example, a gin and tonic at the previous Pepe was 4.50, yesterday it was 10, and the squid rings have risen to 14 euros, worsening the quality and making the portion smaller. On the positive side, the bravas are very good, and the tortilla is still very good. I hope it improves as it is one of the best places in Somo.

reviews.address: Bo. la Estación, S/N, 39794 Hoz de Anero, Cantabria
reviews.review_text: Simply a must-visit spot in the area. The hearty stews (red beans, green beans with clams, beans with duck) are incredible and very generous. The set menu is €14 with wine, water, and bread, and the weekend menu is €22, so going during the week is highly recommended. If you don't want to order from the set menu, a serving of beans is around €10 and is quite filling. I recommend going hungry, as the portions are very, very large.

reviews.address: C. de Almendrales, 24, Usera, 28026 Madrid
reviews.review_text: Excellent service and attention. I will definitely buy again!

reviews.address: C. Galán, 28, 33405 Salinas, Asturias
Av. Rufo García Rendueles, 3, Centro, 33203 Gijón, Asturias
reviews.review_text: A very typical Gijón cider bar, with a highly recommended cachopo. The squid afogaos were excellent. Reservations are required, especially in summer.

reviews.address: C/ Bernardo Álvarez Galán, 14, 33405 Salinas, Asturias
reviews.review_text: Delicious food and excellent service. The best part was the slow-cooked beef tacos and the salad – highly recommended!

reviews.address: C. de Guzmán el Bueno, 62, Chamberí, 28015 Madrid
reviews.review_text: If you are looking for a place where you can train with great teachers, which both engage you on the training and also challenge you, this is the place.
reviews.owner_response: thank you so much Javi, see you soon at boostconcept.

reviews.address: Jardin Compans Caffarelli, Bd Lascrosses, 31000 Toulouse, France

reviews.place_name: Bonne Soirée Restaurants مطعم بون سواريه
reviews.address: 9 Nasr Awad, Ein as Seirah, Old Cairo, Cairo Governorate 4245024, Egypt
reviews.review_text: Completely disgusting. Do not go.

reviews.address: C. de Méndez Álvaro, 74, Arganzuela, 28045 Madrid
reviews.review_text: I'd heard this burger joint was the best in Madrid, and the truth is, I'm not giving it a bad rating because the burger itself isn't good, but because of the value for money and the fries. While the bun and bacon are outstanding, the sauce is lacking; if you order extra sauce, it costs 50 cents more (amazing considering it's a normal Smokey BBQ Mayonnaise), and the meat isn't anything special, enough to warrant paying 12.50 without fries. By comparison, Pinks and La Prensa are better, in my opinion, and are cheaper. The real problem is the fries, which are obviously frozen bagged potato wedges (both Pinks and La Prensa have fries for 4 euros that are very, very good), and the place itself, which is pretty shabby for its prices. We paid 19.50 per person for a burger, fries, and beer, prices you'd get at higher-end places like NY Burger or La Bistroteca. That's why I'm somewhat dissatisfied, since in my opinion, if they charged 15 euros it would be adequate, but it's a bit out of line with what they actually offer. If you want a good smash at a good price, I would recommend Pinks or La Prensa.

reviews.address: Carrer de Bisbe Nadal, 10, 07100 Sóller, Illes Balears
reviews.review_text: The rice with peas mentioned in the previous review was delicious and horrifying. The artichokes are also highly recommended!

reviews.address: Carrer del Pare Vives, 72, 07460 Pollença, Illes Balears
reviews.review_text: Very rich menu, homemade food and at a very good price.

reviews.address: Pg. de Mallorca, 18, Centre, 07012 Palma, Illes Balears
reviews.review_text: Normally, these types of reviews, where you complain about the food arriving late, don't usually reflect well or earn 5 stars. Yesterday, we went to this place for dinner, and two of the dishes arrived 45 minutes late, so much so that we asked to cancel one of them. When we asked to cancel, the waiters politely apologized and readily offered to cancel the meal, buying us a drink. These types of situations are noteworthy, and they make a virtue of the restaurant's human error due to overload. Otherwise, the oxtail croquettes were delicious, the huevos rotos were good, but we were surprised by how delicious the vermouth was.

reviews.address: Plaça del Bisbe Berenguer de Palou, 8, Centre, 07003 Palma, Illes Balears
reviews.review_text: A disgrace. We went on a recommendation, but honestly, I wouldn't go back. The place itself isn't bad, but the service was beyond awful. The waitress didn't know what each dish was, spilled a splash of wine on us and didn't even offer us any, and charged us €7.50 for a pre-made ice cream sundae. The food itself was subpar, and rather expensive considering how shabby the place was (€17 for garlic prawns, €7 for a bread basket for 5, €10 for a plate with 3 slices of sobrasada). If you can save yourself the trouble, better to avoid it.

reviews.address: C. de Colmenares, 7, Centro, 28004 Madrid
C. de Espartinas, 7, Salamanca, 28001 Madrid
reviews.review_text: The best in Madrid. Period.
reviews.owner_response: Hi Javier, we are very grateful for your positive rating and it encourages us to continue providing the best service! Best regards!

reviews.address: C. de Hermosilla, 39, Salamanca, 28001 Madrid
reviews.review_text: A very nice place and excellent service. We ordered a variety of sushi, duck à l'orange, and the oxtail rolls. Highly recommended!

reviews.address: C. de Benito Gutiérrez, 5, Moncloa - Aravaca, 28008 Madrid
reviews.review_text: Spectacular ceviche and unbeatable prices. 100% recommended

reviews.address: Iparragirre K., 13, 20001 Donostia / San Sebastián, Gipuzkoa
reviews.review_text: A very special place. Although the portions aren't huge, the quality of the food is very good, and the flavors aren't typical of the area. The clams and tomato juice are highly recommended.

reviews.address: C. Peñas Blancas, 7, BAJO, 39140 Somo, Cantabria
C. Isla de Mouro, 10, P11, 39140 Somo, Cantabria
reviews.review_text: Despite the nice decor and the fact that some of the food tasted decent, the reason this is a 3-star rating is that the portions are ridiculously small for the price. For example, the Katsu is €16 for 7 chicken and pork fingers with a tiny cup of curry. It's not a traditional katsu with curry and rice; it's more like bar chicken fingers, which weren't bad, but €16 for that portion is excessive. The ribs are much the same: €16 for a small plate of ribs with hardly any side dishes. The concept of the place is good, but €16 for such small portions leaves much to be desired.

reviews.address: C. del Sol, 47, 39003 Santander, Cantabria
reviews.review_text: Excellent service, very good quality, and an unbeatable price. A place with special flavors in Santander.

reviews.address: C. las Quebrantas, 14, 39140 Somo, Cantabria
Calle de Sta Marta, 4, 31005 Pamplona, Navarra
Calle San Cosme y San Damián, 1, 31191 Cordovilla, Navarra
reviews.review_text: My experience at Erreleku can be summed up in two words: rudeness. After asking which dishes contained oats, the waiter completely misunderstood what I was saying. He seemed to think there was something called "wheat oats" and told us, quite grumpily, that I couldn't eat anything on the menu (not even nachos, which are corn tortillas). When I insisted, politely in case he hadn't understood correctly, the waiter pulled his mask down completely and started yelling at us. Every table in the restaurant turned around at the sight of the spectacle he was putting on. When you go to places like this, with their relatively high prices, you expect at least service that matches the cost. In this case, not only was the waiter untrained to know about allergens, but he acted like a clown in front of everyone and wasn't even able to ask any of the other waiters or put me in touch with the head chef. In short, if you're looking for a good meal and want to be treated well, avoid this place.

reviews.address: Henao Kalea, 13, Abando, 48009 Bilbao, Bizkaia
reviews.review_text: Josu is a spectacular chef, and the service is perfect. I highly recommend the salad with foie shavings, the cured meat, and the ribeye steak!

reviews.address: Via Sopramuro, 9, 80142 Napoli NA, Italy
reviews.review_text: Excellent place if you want a good priced pasta with seafood or grilled seafood (9 EUR aprox per dish). The staff is composed by an old Neapolitan couple that is very kind and welcoming. Definitely a must if you want traditional food without being charged like in the lungomare, and if you want to enjoy Neapolitan home made cooking.

reviews.address: C. Cataluña, 6, 31006 Pamplona, Navarra
reviews.review_text: Good bar, good food and perfect service

reviews.address: C. Caballeros Templarios, 0, 31550 Ribaforada, Navarra
General Artetxe Kalea, 6, 20002 Donostia / San Sebastián, Gipuzkoa
reviews.review_text: They overcharged us and didn't even listen to us afterwards

reviews.address: 22 Rue Rambuteau, 75003 Paris, France
reviews.review_text: Great price for Paris. 13 euros for a burger, drink, and fries. Highly recommended.

reviews.address: Calle del Príncipe de Vergara, 268, Chamartín, 28016 Madrid
reviews.review_text:
It's supposed to be an Italian restaurant, but they have less Italian than Telepizza.

Very expensive if you don't have the 2-for-1 deal. Another rip-off from the Vips group, they really screwed us over!

reviews.address: Edificio Cultural Tomas Y Valiente, C/ de Leganés, 51, Local 8, 28945 Fuenlabrada, Madrid
reviews.review_text:
Be careful, they might even put oregano in your beer.

It took half an hour to order three beers; the waiters were clueless, like they'd been abducted by a UFO.
reviews.owner_response:
Hahahaha... well, maybe we were under a UFO that day and we were a little distracted...

Seriously, maybe we didn't have a good day, and we apologize for that.

reviews.address: Domstraße 82, 50668 Köln, Germany
reviews.review_text: Extremely expensive restaurant on weekends. The owners do not change the menu outside, and when they give you the menu inside, you receive an unpleasant surprise.

reviews.address: Pl. del Dos de Mayo, 4, Centro, 28004 Madrid
reviews.review_text: After waiting 30 minutes for a table on the terrace, they let in 4 groups before us without any reservation.

reviews.address: Pl. Grande, 10, 06300 Zafra, Badajoz
reviews.review_text: Very good hotel, but the bathroom was a bit under-cleaned. They forgot to mention in the reservation that the hotel parking costs around 20 euros.

reviews.address: Boticas, 12, 06300 Zafra, Badajoz
reviews.review_text: Original, good quality, and good prices. Highly recommended.

reviews.address: Beco do Sol 3, 8200-269 Albufeira, Portugal
reviews.review_text: Best place to eat in the whole area. Fish quality is 10/10 and prices are very good (paid 47 euro for 3 dishes, wine and water).

reviews.address: Pl. Cervantes, 16, 41640 Osuna, Sevilla
reviews.review_text: Top restaurant for a low price. Original and delicious cuisine. Recommended stop on every route.

reviews.address: Calle del Gral. Pardiñas, 24, Salamanca, 28001 Madrid
reviews.review_text: Very good value for money menu. The craft beers were very good. I recommend the croquettes and the burrata salad. The reason for the one less star is the slow service and the dissatisfaction of some diners with the ramen soup, which was basically salt water.

reviews.address: C. Principal de Provincias, 8, Moncloa - Aravaca, 28011 Madrid
reviews.review_text:
A disappointing meal.

The entrecote was only cooked on one side, and the salad was drenched in oil. Since it was a long weekend, they must have prepared the food the day before, even the entrecote. It was a complete letdown. I didn't ask for a refund out of embarrassment, but I should have. The dessert was a poorly made, three-day-old cake. The only redeeming feature was the ham. I recommend that anyone considering going save themselves the trip and try other restaurants in the area.

reviews.address: Freudpl. 2, 1020 Wien, Austria
reviews.review_text: Pizza was certainly good and big sized. Though somethings like carbonara with cream showed that this is not a traditional italian restaurant, this place deserves to be visited, because of its quality/price.
reviews.owner_response: Ciao Javier, grazie mille for your fantastic feedback! <3 Come and visit us again soon! Kind regards, your L'Osteria Team

reviews.address: C. del Limón, 9, Centro, 28015 Madrid
reviews.review_text: A great place to grab some tapas at a decent price. Soft drinks and beer are cheap, and the typical tapas, while not the best quality, are still worth it.

reviews.address: C. de Sta. Lucrecia, 10, Carabanchel, 28019 Madrid
reviews.owner_response:
Good afternoon Javier,

Thank you very much for your rating. Best regards :)

reviews.address: Calle de Don Ramón de la Cruz, 48, Salamanca, 28001 Madrid
reviews.review_text: Mexican food at exorbitant prices. There are much better places in Madrid with better prices, and places for the same price that are far superior. The restaurant should reconsider its menu prices given the quality they offer. To give you an idea: soft drinks are €3, three tacos are €9.60, and nachos al pastor with guacamole are €14.60.

reviews.address: C. del Ave María, 19, Centro, 28012 Madrid
reviews.review_text: I had high expectations for this place, and in terms of the quality/price ratio of the pizzas, it more than met them. The pizzas are perfect for one person, perhaps a tad thinner than traditional Neapolitan pizza, but you can tell the dough is made properly, both in flavor and texture. The problem arose when they told us they didn't accept reservations, but not to worry, that if we arrived at 9:30 there would be a table. When we arrived at 9:15, there were no tables for six available. Since there were six of us, we had to wait an hour and fifteen minutes instead of the half hour they initially told us, as three tables for two and one for three were served before us, even though they weren't ahead of us on the waiting list. Between that and the extra 15 minutes it took the waitress to serve us, we were a little disappointed. Some people were a bit surprised because both waitresses were Italian and didn't understand certain things when taking our order, but it wasn't a big deal. Even though we had to wait an hour and a half to order our pizzas (I repeat, our fault also for not arriving much earlier) I have given the rating 4 stars because I was impressed with this quality-price ratio in an authentic pizzeria in Madrid (a real Neapolitan margherita is cheaper than a medium one from Telepizza).

reviews.address: Stockport Rd, Manchester M12 6AP, United Kingdom
reviews.review_text: Optimum place to see gigs. The place has cloakroom and free water on the circle bar.

reviews.place_name: Liberty Supper Club | Night Club Madrid
reviews.address: Calle de Juan Bravo, 31, Salamanca, 28006 Madrid
P.º de la Castellana, 91, Tetuán, 28046 Madrid
reviews.review_text: Good burgers, a little greasy in my opinion, and not very big. That's why it's only worth going on Thursdays, to take advantage of the 2-for-1 offer. The salads are quite good for the price, and the soft drinks are refillable. The beer selection is good, but unfortunately they serve Cruzcampo as the standard beer.

reviews.address: Pl. Mayor, Centro, 28012 Madrid
1 Richmond St, Liverpool L1 1EE, United Kingdom
reviews.review_text: Prices are 1 quid more than on some menu photos (as of autumn 2017 small 5£, medium 6£ and big 7£, spring rolls 3£). Small is enough for one and the big one is enough for two. There is a wide variety of spicyness, we had kare chicken, and it was really nice, a bit more spicy than we thought, but with a 1£ coke can we could eat it. Totally recommended for having lunch on the go and for tourists.

reviews.address: Trumpeldor St 11, Tel Aviv-Yafo, Israel
reviews.review_text: OK hotel in the beach of Tel Aviv. Some rooms do not have beach views, while others have. Breakfast is not very good and the size of the rooms is quite disappointing, but the service and location are quite acceptable. Due to the high price, the review should be 2.5 stars.

reviews.address: Carr. de Dénia A Xábia, 100, 03700 Dénia, Alicante
reviews.review_text: Creative and unique food. An excellent place to eat in Denia for its quality, price, and uniqueness. The restaurant presents a fusion of cuisines that changes seasonally, this year featuring Mexican, Peruvian, and Mediterranean cuisine. For three starters, three main courses, three desserts, and drinks for four people, the bill came to 92 euros, including the all-you-can-eat offer. At 23 euros per person, you'd be hard pressed to find such a good meal anywhere else. Highly recommended.

reviews.address: Assagador del Palmar, 22, 03700 Dénia, Alicante
reviews.review_text: Decent quality (for an Italian restaurant in Spain, save for the insult to dishes like carbonara, capricciosa, and Bolognese), good prices, but TERRIBLE service. Total disarray when taking notes, loud reggae music, and pizzas that are 20 minutes late. Don't order the patatas bravas, as they're the Mercadona style, deep-fried and served with barbecue sauce, not even brava sauce. The pizzas are decent and large, but, like the pasta, they're typical of an attempted Italian restaurant in Spain. Beer and wine are reasonably priced, and when they make mistakes or are late (which, unfortunately, is often), they usually give you something for free.

reviews.address: Via de' Musei, 2/4, 40124 Bologna BO, Italy
reviews.review_text: Expensive and not the best place to eat. The location is nice, but the food is lacking. There are plenty of better places than this restaurant in Bologna.

reviews.review_text: Restaurant for locals = epic win. Food was fantastic, service too, and the price with entrees, main course and desert (including very little wine) was of about 25 eur each. Totally recommend to have lunch here instead of waiting 1 h for a table in the lungomare.

reviews.address: Via Partenope, V. Francesco Caracciolo, 48, 80121 Napoli NA, Italy
reviews.review_text: Breakfast is fantastic and service is 10/10. The only problem for the price is that it doesn't include parking.

reviews.address: Via Santa Lucia, 56, 80132 Napoli NA, Italy
reviews.review_text: Pizza and mozzarella are top notch, but they didn't respect our reservation and charged 2,50 for service per person. Shame that things like that bring down one of the best pizzerias in Napoli.

reviews.address: Naples, Metropolitan City of Naples, Italy
reviews.review_text: Ferries to Capri are a rip-off: 20 euros each way.

reviews.address: Piazza Navona, 00186 Roma RM, Italy
Via Giovanni Giolitti, 40, 00185 Rome, RM, Italy
P.za del Colosseo, 1, 00184 Roma RM, Italy
reviews.review_text: Beautiful place, but very very crowded and with long queues.

reviews.address: 00186 Rome, Metropolitan City of Rome Capital, Italy
Via Santa Maria in Via, 19, 00187 Roma RM, Italy
reviews.review_text: I've gone there two times to eat on the go and I'm surprised of the low prices taking into account that the place is 3 min walking from the Fontana di Trevi. Sandwitches are very big (a little bit smaller than the ones in the antico vinaio from Florence), and are delicious, ranging from 4.50 eur to 6 (these being the same size, the only thing that varies is the inside). A bottle of water costs 1 eur and other beverages are too expensive. I personally reccomend the sandwitch D for 4.50 (pesto, brie, turkey and zucchini) or the sandwitch P for 6 eur. With one of these sandwiches, a normal person can eat and be satiated. For allergics, the bread does not contain oats and is made from "grano saraceno" and corn, though, they do not assure you it might not contain other cereals.

reviews.address: Via della Stazione Tuscolana, 142, 00182 Roma RM, Italy
reviews.review_text: The food is cheap, but the pizza is nothing good and the pasta is usually acceptable.

reviews.address: Piazza dela Vittoria, 5, 80071 Anacapri NA, Italy
reviews.review_text: Good, but for tourists. This restaurant takes advantage of tour groups, as so does the rest of Capri. Pasta for lunch is meh and pizza is fairly basic. Overall, if you need to eat something, it does the job, but as the rest of Capri, it is for tourists. We had salad+dish+drink+dessert for 14€, which was quite ok, but the quality was a little bit poor.

reviews.address: Via Carlo Alberto, 15, 80045 Pompei NA, Italy
reviews.review_text:
€100 for five people for second-rate food

The meat was acceptable, as was the pizza, but the appetizer was poor quality, the pasta wasn't fresh (the baked pasta was cold), and the fish was frozen. If you want truly good food for the price, look elsewhere.

reviews.place_name: BoXinBox Music - Rehearsal Studios in Madrid
reviews.address: C. de Sánchez Pacheco, 82, Chamartín, 28002 Madrid
reviews.review_text: The best place to rehearse by the hour in Madrid. I've tried five other places in Madrid, and these are undoubtedly the best. I've been rehearsing here for three years. The service and equipment are unbeatable. The rooms have a wide variety of backing tracks, which can be adjusted to suit the needs of the band, both in terms of musical style and the space they require. Boxinbox also has a bar where you can have a beer before or after rehearsal, and you can even take it with you inside the studio.

reviews.address: P.º de la Castellana, 130, Chamartín, 28046 Madrid
reviews.review_text: Excellent value for money. The menu features small, very original dishes ranging from 4 to 7 euros. For lunch, we recommend choosing between 2 and 4 per person. Most dishes have an oriental touch. We recommend the seasonal anchovies, the wagyu and chicken skewers, and choosing a rice dish to accompany them. The restaurant is beautifully decorated, very quiet, and the service is excellent. Drinks are quite expensive, so instead of bottles of water, we recommend ordering jugs. The price is around 10-20 euros per person, depending on what you eat and whether you choose a drink. Reservations are recommended well in advance.

reviews.review_text: Good place to stay in the center of Bologna. Clean place, confortable bed, big and includes full kitchen with coffee. Service is outstanding. The price is around 260 euro three nights, for 2 people, but 3 can sleep perfectly, so, its much better than a regular hotel.

reviews.review_text: Excellent coffee and desserts. The price for a coffee with a couple of desserts is 3-4 euros (it's not the cheapest, but the place is not bad either). All the products are handmade, and the Christmas selection is delicious.

reviews.review_text: Excellent service and delicious food. The only problem is the price, €30-40 per person. The location is excellent and ideal for eating out, opposite the Church of Santo Stefano.

reviews.address: C/ del Marqués de Campo, 58, 03700 Dénia, Alicante
reviews.review_text: Expensive prices and incompetence. A run-of-the-mill two-scoop ice cream for €3.40. The speculoos ice cream doesn't taste like speculoos; it's vanilla with cinnamon. The yogurt ice cream is just average, though it's nicely presented. I also asked for nutritional information, as I'm allergic to oats, and despite having a sign on the wall stating they have ALL the nutritional information available, they only knew whether it contained gluten or not. As soon as I asked, both employees, who were Argentinian, became rude to me, almost accusing me of asking why I was asking when I told them I was allergic. It's the obligation of all establishments to have the nutritional information for their products, and these people not only failed to provide it but also treated me with contempt. For this reason, I won't be returning to this ice cream shop, and I recommend everyone walk 100 meters further to the original ice cream shop on Marqués de Campo.
reviews.owner_response:
Thank you for taking the time to share your opinion. We would like to clarify a few details: We have an Italian-influenced ice cream tradition, therefore, we serve our ice cream with a scoop, not a ball. You felt that the speculoos flavor contained vanilla, but this essence is not present in the recipe; it is a cookie imported from Germany. The same applies to the yogurt flavor; your taste buds may find it more or less appealing. Regarding your request for information—as you correctly stated—you claimed to be allergic to oats, and we informed you accordingly.

Regarding the allergen declaration under Regulation 1169/2011, OATS are not included. We also suggest that, for your safety, you try any of our sorbets, as no third-party companies are involved in their production or packaging. The allergen list for each flavor is available.

On the other hand, one of the things that links our two nations (Spain and Argentina) is the use of the Spanish language, but not necessarily our idioms. Therefore, what might have sounded "cool" to you is simply our colloquial use of the language. We apologize if you felt offended or hurt by this, but it was not our intention.
We are pleased that you will choose our colleagues in the sector next time, as they are excellent at what they do.
Thank you for your time in helping us improve. Sincerely.

reviews.review_text: After taking 30 minutes to bring our food, we were treated to a round of free tapas and drinks, which was a real plus, as not many restaurants and bars offer this. The tapas and portions are of very good quality, and the price (at least for non-locals) is reasonable.

reviews.address: C. de Alburquerque, 8, Chamberí, 28010 Madrid
reviews.review_text: Reheated food, at the price of a good restaurant. The wine was quite good, but the fact that they charge you €8 for reheated rice that tastes like nothing is a must-try. The croquettes were more of the same.

reviews.address: C. del Cardenal Cisneros, 19, Chamberí, 28010 Madrid
reviews.review_text: A very good restaurant. It's not expensive, and the dishes are very original. Much better than other restaurants in the area, which are similarly priced. The atmosphere is very relaxed.

reviews.address: Calle de Fuencarral, 116, Chamberí, 28010 Madrid
reviews.review_text: Very good value for money. The problem is that their selection has been getting worse.

reviews.address: C. de Guzmán el Bueno, 43, Chamberí, 28015 Madrid
reviews.review_text: An hour waiting for help, two hours and twenty minutes to replace the cover of the phone, and the worst part is that they reset the phone to factory settings... All just to replace a cover. Save yourself the trouble and send it to Sony.
